docs.text: TOPIC: Algebra SOURCE: JEE Math Knowledge Base === QUADRATIC EQUATIONS === Standard form: ax² + bx + c = 0 Quadratic formula: x = (-b ± √(b²-4ac)) / 2a Discriminant: D = b² - 4ac - D > 0: two distinct real roots - D = 0: two equal real roots - D < 0: no real roots (complex roots) Sum of roots: α + β = -b/a Product of roots: αβ = c/a Nature of roots: - If D is a perfect square → rational roots - If D > 0 and not perfect square → irrational roots === POLYNOMIALS === Factor theorem: (x-a) is a factor of p(x) if p(a) = 0 Remainder theorem: When p(x) is divided by (x-a), remainder = p(a) Vieta's formulas for cubic ax³+bx²+cx+d=0: - Sum of roots: α+β+γ = -b/a - Sum of products two at a time: αβ+βγ+γα = c/a - Product of roots: αβγ = -d/a === SEQUENCES AND SERIES === Arithmetic Progression (AP): - General term: aₙ = a + (n-1)d - Sum of n terms: Sₙ = n/2 × [2a + (n-1)d] - Sum of n terms: Sₙ = n/2 × (a + l) where l = last term Geometric Progression (GP): - General term: aₙ = ar^(n-1) - Sum of n terms: Sₙ = a(rⁿ-1)/(r-1) for r≠1 - Sum of infinite GP: S∞ = a/(1-r) for |r| < 1 Harmonic Progression (HP): - General term: 1/aₙ is in AP - HM of a and b: H = 2ab/(a+b) AM-GM-HM inequality: AM ≥ GM ≥ HM === INEQUALITIES === AM-GM: (a+b)/2 ≥ √(ab) for positive a,b Cauchy-Schwarz: (a²+b²)(c²+d²) ≥ (ac+bd)² === BINOMIAL THEOREM === (a+b)ⁿ = Σ C(n,r) × aⁿ⁻ʳ × bʳ, r from 0 to n General term: Tᵣ₊₁ = C(n,r) × aⁿ⁻ʳ × bʳ Middle term: If n is even, middle term = T_(n/2+1) === LOGARITHMS === logₐ(mn) = logₐm + logₐn logₐ(m/n) = logₐm - logₐn logₐ(mⁿ) = n × logₐm Change of base: logₐb = log b / log a logₐa = 1, logₐ1 = 0 === COMPLEX NUMBERS === i = √(-1), i² = -1, i³ = -i, i⁴ = 1 |z| = √(a²+b²) for z = a+bi Argument: θ = arctan(b/a) De Moivre: (cosθ + i sinθ)ⁿ = cos(nθ) + i sin(nθ) Euler: e^(iθ) = cosθ + i sinθ === COMMON MISTAKES IN ALGEBRA === - Forgetting ± when taking square roots -

logₐ(mⁿ) = n × logₐm Change of base: logₐb = log b / log a logₐa = 1, logₐ1 = 0 === COMPLEX NUMBERS === i = √(-1), i² = -1, i³ = -i, i⁴ = 1 |z| = √(a²+b²) for z = a+bi Argument: θ = arctan(b/a) De Moivre: (cosθ + i sinθ)ⁿ = cos(nθ) + i sin(nθ) Euler: e^(iθ) = cosθ + i sinθ === COMMON MISTAKES IN ALGEBRA === - Forgetting ± when taking square roots - Sign errors when expanding (a-b)² = a² - 2ab + b² NOT a² + b² - (a+b)² ≠ a² + b² (must include 2ab) - Division by zero when variable is in denominator - log of negative numbers is undefined in real domain

TOPIC: Calculus - Limits, Derivatives, Integration SOURCE: JEE Math Knowledge Base === LIMITS === Standard limits: lim(x→0) sin(x)/x = 1 lim(x→0) tan(x)/x = 1 lim(x→0) (1-cos x)/x² = 1/2 lim(x→∞) (1 + 1/x)^x = e lim(x→0) (1 + x)^(1/x) = e lim(x→0) (eˣ - 1)/x = 1 lim(x→0) (aˣ - 1)/x = ln(a) lim(x→0) ln(1+x)/x = 1 L'Hôpital's Rule: If lim f(x)/g(x) = 0/0 or ∞/∞ form: → lim f(x)/g(x) = lim f'(x)/g'(x) Squeeze theorem: If g(x) ≤ f(x) ≤ h(x) and lim g = lim h = L → lim f = L === DERIVATIVES === Basic rules: d/dx(xⁿ) = nxⁿ⁻¹ d/dx(sin x) = cos x d/dx(cos x) = -sin x d/dx(tan x) = sec²x d/dx(eˣ) = eˣ d/dx(ln x) = 1/x d/dx(aˣ) = aˣ ln(a) d/dx(constant) = 0 Chain rule: d/dx[f(g(x))] = f'(g(x)) × g'(x) Product rule: d/dx[f×g] = f'g + fg' Quotient rule: d/dx[f/g] = (f'g - fg')/g² Implicit differentiation: differentiate both sides, solve for dy/dx === APPLICATIONS OF DERIVATIVES === Increasing function: f'(x) > 0 Decreasing function: f'(x) < 0 Local maxima: f'(x) = 0 and f''(x) < 0 Local minima: f'(x) = 0 and f''(x) > 0 Inflection point: f''(x) = 0 Tangent slope at x=a: m = f'(a) Tangent line: y - f(a) = f'(a)(x - a) Normal line: y - f(a) = -1/f'(a) × (x - a) === INTEGRATION === ∫xⁿ dx = xⁿ⁺¹/(n+1) + C [n ≠ -1] ∫1/x dx = ln|x| + C ∫eˣ dx = eˣ + C ∫sin x dx = -cos x + C ∫cos x dx = sin x + C ∫sec²x dx = tan x + C ∫aˣ dx = aˣ/ln(a) + C Integration by parts: ∫u dv = uv - ∫v du (LIATE rule for u) LIATE: Logarithmic, Inverse trig, Algebraic, Trigonometric, Exponential Definite integral: ∫[a to b] f(x)dx = F(b) - F(a) Area between curves: ∫[a to b] |f(x) - g(x)| dx === COMMON MISTAKES IN CALCULUS === - Forgetting +C in indefinite integrals - Wrong sign in d/dx(cos x) = -sin x (not +sin x) - Not applying chain rule when differentiating composite functions - Confusing when to use product rule vs chain rule - L'Hopital only applies to 0/0 or ∞/∞ forms

MISTAKES IN CALCULUS === - Forgetting +C in indefinite integrals - Wrong sign in d/dx(cos x) = -sin x (not +sin x) - Not applying chain rule when differentiating composite functions - Confusing when to use product rule vs chain rule - L'Hopital only applies to 0/0 or ∞/∞ forms

TOPIC: Linear Algebra Basics SOURCE: JEE Math Knowledge Base === MATRICES === Types: Row, Column, Square, Diagonal, Identity (I), Zero, Symmetric, Skew-symmetric Transpose: (Aᵀ)ᵢⱼ = Aⱼᵢ (AB)ᵀ = BᵀAᵀ (A+B)ᵀ = Aᵀ + Bᵀ === DETERMINANTS === 2×2: |A| = ad - bc for [[a,b],[c,d]] 3×3: Expand along first row using cofactors Properties: - |AB| = |A||B| - |Aᵀ| = |A| - If two rows/cols are identical → det = 0 - Swapping two rows → det changes sign - Multiplying a row by k → det multiplied by k - |kA| = kⁿ|A| for n×n matrix === INVERSE OF A MATRIX === A⁻¹ = adj(A) / |A| AA⁻¹ = A⁻¹A = I (AB)⁻¹ = B⁻¹A⁻¹ Inverse exists only when |A| ≠ 0 (non-singular) === SYSTEM OF LINEAR EQUATIONS === AX = B - Unique solution: |A| ≠ 0 → X = A⁻¹B - No solution: |A| = 0 and B is not consistent - Infinite solutions: |A| = 0 and system is consistent Cramer's Rule: x = Dₓ/D, y = D_y/D, z = D_z/D [where D = |A|] === VECTORS === |a| = √(a₁² + a₂² + a₃²) Dot product: a·b = |a||b|cos θ = a₁b₁ + a₂b₂ + a₃b₃ Cross product: |a×b| = |a||b|sin θ a·b = 0 → perpendicular vectors a×b = 0 → parallel vectors Projection of a on b: (a·b)/|b| Unit vector: â = a/|a| === RANK === Rank = number of non-zero rows in row echelon form For AX = B: - rank(A) = rank(augmented) = n → unique solution - rank(A) = rank(augmented) < n → infinite solutions - rank(A) ≠ rank(augmented) → no solution === EIGENVALUES === det(A - λI) = 0 → characteristic equation Sum of eigenvalues = trace(A) Product of eigenvalues = det(A) === COMMON MISTAKES IN LINEAR ALGEBRA === - Matrix multiplication is NOT commutative: AB ≠ BA in general - (A+B)² ≠ A² + 2AB + B² (because AB ≠ BA) - Can't divide by a matrix — must multiply by inverse - Determinant of 3×3 needs careful sign pattern (+,-,+)
AB ≠ BA) - Can't divide by a matrix — must multiply by inverse - Determinant of 3×3 needs careful sign pattern (+,-,+)

TOPIC: Probability SOURCE: JEE Math Knowledge Base === BASIC PROBABILITY === P(A) = (Favorable outcomes) / (Total outcomes) Range: 0 ≤ P(A) ≤ 1 P(A') = 1 - P(A) [complement] P(A ∪ B) = P(A) + P(B) - P(A ∩ B) P(A ∩ B) = P(A) × P(B) [if A and B are independent] === CONDITIONAL PROBABILITY === P(A|B) = P(A ∩ B) / P(B), P(B) ≠ 0 P(A ∩ B) = P(B) × P(A|B) = P(A) × P(B|A) === BAYES' THEOREM === P(A|B) = [P(B|A) × P(A)] / P(B) Law of Total Probability: P(B) = Σ P(B|Aᵢ) × P(Aᵢ) === COUNTING PRINCIPLES === Permutation: P(n,r) = n! / (n-r)! [ORDER MATTERS] Combination: C(n,r) = n! / [r!(n-r)!] [ORDER DOESN'T MATTER] C(n,r) = C(n, n-r) C(n,0) = C(n,n) = 1 Multiplication principle: If event A can occur in m ways AND event B in n ways → m×n ways Addition principle: If event A can occur in m ways OR event B in n ways → m+n ways === RANDOM VARIABLES === Expected value: E(X) = Σ xᵢ × P(xᵢ) Variance: Var(X) = E(X²) - [E(X)]² Standard deviation: σ = √Var(X) E(aX+b) = aE(X) + b Var(aX+b) = a²Var(X) === BINOMIAL DISTRIBUTION === X ~ B(n, p): n trials, probability p of success P(X=r) = C(n,r) × pʳ × (1-p)^(n-r) E(X) = np Var(X) = np(1-p) σ = √(np(1-p)) === GEOMETRIC DISTRIBUTION === P(X=k) = p × (1-p)^(k-1) [first success on kth trial] E(X) = 1/p === IMPORTANT RESULTS === Mutually exclusive events: P(A ∩ B) = 0 Exhaustive events: P(A ∪ B) = 1 Independent events: P(A ∩ B) = P(A) × P(B) === COMMON MISTAKES IN PROBABILITY === - Confusing permutation and combination - Forgetting to subtract when using inclusion-exclusion - Confusing "at least one" with "exactly one" → P(at least 1) = 1 - P(none) - Not checking if events are mutually exclusive before adding - Confusing independent events with mutually exclusive events

TOPIC: Solution Templates and Problem-Solving Patterns SOURCE: JEE Math Knowledge Base === SOLVING QUADRATIC EQUATIONS: TEMPLATE === Step 1: Write in standard form ax² + bx + c = 0 Step 2: Calculate discriminant D = b² - 4ac Step 3: If D ≥ 0, apply quadratic formula x = (-b ± √D) / 2a Step 4: Simplify roots Step 5: Verify by substituting back Step 6: State answer with domain constraints === SOLVING PROBABILITY PROBLEMS: TEMPLATE === Step 1: Identify sample space S and find n(S) Step 2: Identify the event A Step 3: Count favorable outcomes n(A) Step 4: Apply P(A) = n(A)/n(S) Step 5: Check if events are independent or mutually exclusive Step 6: Use addition/multiplication rules as needed Step 7: Verify answer is between 0 and 1 === FINDING MAXIMA/MINIMA: TEMPLATE === Step 1: Define the objective function f(x) Step 2: Find f'(x) Step 3: Set f'(x) = 0, solve for critical points Step 4: Find f''(x) Step 5: If f''(x) < 0 → maxima; f''(x) > 0 → minima Step 6: Evaluate f at critical points and boundary points Step 7: Compare to find global max/min === INTEGRATION STRATEGY: TEMPLATE === Step 1: Look for direct formula match Step 2: Try substitution (u-sub): set u = inner function Step 3: Try integration by parts if product: ∫u dv = uv - ∫v du Step 4: For rational functions: partial fractions Step 5: For trig: use identities to simplify Step 6: Add +C for indefinite integrals Step 7: For definite: evaluate at bounds === PERMUTATION vs COMBINATION DECISION GUIDE === Ask: Does ORDER matter? YES → Permutation P(n,r) = n!/(n-r)! NO → Combination C(n,r) = n!/[r!(n-r)!] Keywords for Permutation: arrange, order, rank, sequence Keywords for Combination: select, choose, pick, group, committee === COMMON JEE PROBLEM PATTERNS === Pattern 1: "At least one" → Use complement: 1 - P(none) Pattern 2: "Exactly k out of n" → Binomial distribution C(n,k)p^k(1-p)^(n-k) Pattern 3: "Sum of digits" → AP formula Pattern 4: "Product of roots" → Vieta's formulas Pattern 5: "Rate of change" → Derivatives Pattern 6: "Area under curve" → Definite integral Pattern 7: "Maximum profit / minimum cost" → Optimization via derivatives Pattern 8: "n people in a row" → n! permutations Pattern 9: "Committee of r from n" → C(n,r) combinations Pattern 10: "Divisibility" → Modular arithmetic or factor theorem

Binomial distribution C(n,k)p^k(1-p)^(n-k) Pattern 3: "Sum of digits" → AP formula Pattern 4: "Product of roots" → Vieta's formulas Pattern 5: "Rate of change" → Derivatives Pattern 6: "Area under curve" → Definite integral Pattern 7: "Maximum profit / minimum cost" → Optimization via derivatives Pattern 8: "n people in a row" → n! permutations Pattern 9: "Committee of r from n" → C(n,r) combinations Pattern 10: "Divisibility" → Modular arithmetic or factor theorem
